task.title: List every persisted task deterministically
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/490 by adding a read-only task discovery query over authoritative event histories without introducing a projection table, pagination, runtime/provider API, child tasks, priority, or identity and tool policy.
acceptance_criteria: An empty store returns no tasks, while a populated store returns every task in ascending identifier order with its latest goal, lifecycle status, terminal detail, and optional session association.
Listing is unchanged after reopening the database, excludes session streams with matching external identifiers, and appends no events.
Malformed payloads, invalid histories, and malformed owning task stream identifiers remain explicit errors rather than being skipped.
Discovery and full stream replay use one SQLite read snapshot, and load-by-identifier and listing share the same projection logic.
Documentation, independent review, the local repository gate, required hosted checks, review correction, and squash merge complete successfully.

attempts.summary: Created bounded issue 490 and wrote task listing acceptance tests before production support.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused RED suite failed to compile because TaskStore had no list method and TaskStoreError had no InvalidStreamId variant.
attempts.patch: Added integration scenarios for an empty store, deterministic ordering, latest terminal and session-association state, database reopen, isolation from session streams, no-write behavior, malformed payloads, and malformed stream identities.

attempts.summary: Implemented event-based task discovery and reused task history projection.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused suite passed 35 tests after selecting every stream marked by task.started, validating owning task stream identifiers, projecting full histories, and sorting tasks by opaque identifier bytes.
attempts.patch: Added TaskStore::list, TaskStoreError::InvalidStreamId, shared task projection logic, observable-contract documentation, and the README milestone entry.

attempts.summary: Completed independent pre-commit review and the local quality gate.
attempts.diagnostic: The pinned Codex review reported no findings, and just verify passed formatting, workspace checks, 117 tests, Clippy with warnings denied, SecretSpec integration, and diff checks.
attempts.patch: Kept the implementation aligned with the existing one-snapshot session listing pattern and committed it using a Conventional Commit.

attempts.summary: Addressed CodeRabbit's documentation finding and reverified the branch.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit identified one minor omission: the list contract did not explicitly name InvalidStreamId for malformed owning task stream identities. The finding was valid, fixed, acknowledged, and resolved; the repeated local and hosted gates passed.
attempts.patch: Extended the observable contract with the explicit InvalidStreamId result, committed the correction, and pushed it to pull request 491.

attempts.summary: Squash-merged and synchronized the task discovery capability.
attempts.diagnostic: Pull request 491 merged as 8fbca64b1b7fa3b04fa0d210f0dc4ec98649a085 after hosted Rust quality gate run 29463392567 and CodeRabbit passed, and local main fast-forwarded to the merge commit.
attempts.patch: Linked issue 490, enabled squash auto-merge, monitored both required statuses, and restored the local repository to synchronized main before recording the episode.

outcome.summary: Vela can now discover every persisted task in deterministic order from one consistent read snapshot while replaying authoritative histories, preserving terminal and session-association state, and surfacing corrupt task data explicitly.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle # RED: compile failure because list and InvalidStreamId did not exist
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle # GREEN: 35 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # no findings
nix develop --command just verify # formatting, checking, 117 workspace tests, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff gates passed before and after review correction
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29463392567 and required CodeRabbit status for pull request 491 # passed after the review correction

lessons: Task discovery can use the existing start discriminator as a durable index seed without adding a second mutable projection.
A collection query over event-sourced aggregates should consume complete histories from one read snapshot rather than discover identifiers and replay each stream separately.
Projection logic shared by load-by-identifier and collection queries prevents lifecycle ordering and corruption behavior from drifting.
Stream namespaces isolate task and session aggregates even when their public identifiers are equal.
Collection tests should verify not only ordering and reopen stability but also latest terminal details, optional associations, and absence of writes.
Automated review findings that clarify an observable error contract are worth fixing even when implementation and tests already enforce the behavior.
After a review correction, both local verification and required hosted checks should run again before merge evidence is claimed.
